subtitle: Front of thigh — heavy after downhills and long runs

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Quad soreness peaks 24–48h after long runs with significant downhill

mild.description: DOMS in the quads 1–2 days after a long or hilly run
mild.can_run_note: Yes — easy pace only until soreness resolves
why: Eccentric muscle damage from downhill running — quads brake at every step
Delayed onset muscle soreness from long run volume
VMO fatigue from sustained knee flexion under load
relief: Easy walking or light cycling to promote blood flow
Foam roll quads slowly — pause on tender spots
Cold water immersion or ice bath within 2 hours of run
Protein intake within 30 min of finishing to aid repair
prevention: Gradual exposure to downhill running — introduce 10% grade at a time
Eccentric quad exercises: slow step-downs 3 × 15 each leg
Strength training: leg press and split squats weekly
Nutrition: carbohydrate fuelling during runs over 90 min

moderate.description: Quad pain during runs, weakness on stairs, pain to touch
moderate.can_run_note: Flat easy runs only — no downhills or speed work
why: Rectus femoris strain — the only quad muscle that crosses the hip
Quad muscle tear from sudden acceleration or sprint
relief: RICE for first 48 hours if acute strain
Isometric quad sets: tighten quad with leg straight, hold 10s × 10
prevention: Warm up thoroughly before any speed work
Never sprint from cold — build pace gradually
Strength work: terminal knee extensions and leg press

severe.description: Acute sharp pain, swelling, cannot straighten leg against resistance
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible significant muscle tear
why: Grade 2 or 3 rectus femoris tear requiring imaging
Significant quad strain with haematoma formation
Avulsion at the AIIS in athletes under 25
relief: Ultrasound or MRI to grade the injury
prevention: Progressive return to running over 6–12 weeks depending on grade
Full strength restoration before return to speed work

see_physio_if: Visible swelling or bruising on the thigh
Cannot fully extend the knee against gravity
A pop or snap was felt during injury
Pain not improving after 5 days of rest
